notes: Repo-specific planning labels such as bug, feature, or good first issue remain user-managed.
Vigilante should not add or remove work-classification labels automatically.

labels.name: vigilante:queued
labels.description: The issue is eligible for dispatch and waiting for a worker slot.
notes: Set when Vigilante decides the issue is ready to run but has not started execution yet.
Clear when execution begins or when the issue becomes ineligible.

labels.name: vigilante:running
labels.description: A coding-agent session is currently executing for the issue.
notes: Replaces vigilante:queued while a worktree session is active.

labels.name: vigilante:blocked
labels.description: Execution cannot continue until a blocker is resolved.
notes: Pair with exactly one vigilante:needs-* label when the next step is known.

labels.name: vigilante:ready-for-review
labels.description: Implementation is complete enough for a human to review the resulting PR or branch.
notes: Preferred first-class review handoff label.

labels.name: vigilante:awaiting-user-validation
labels.description: Changes are ready for product or operator validation before the issue is considered done.
notes: Use when code review is complete but a human still needs to validate behavior.

labels.name: vigilante:done
labels.description: Vigilante completed its work on the issue and no further automation is expected.
notes: Typically set after merge, explicit operator completion, or another terminal success condition.

labels.name: vigilante:needs-human-input
labels.description: The agent is waiting on product, operator, or repository-owner guidance.
notes: Use for ambiguous requirements, missing decisions, or manual approvals.

labels.name: vigilante:needs-provider-fix
labels.description: Execution is blocked by provider auth, quota, or runtime setup issues.
notes: Maps naturally from existing blocked provider failure classes.

labels.name: vigilante:needs-git-fix
labels.description: Execution is blocked by repository or git state that requires human repair.
notes: Use for auth failures, dirty worktrees, rebase conflicts, or branch repair.

labels.name: codex
labels.description: Routes the issue to the Codex provider for execution.
notes: Existing control behavior remains unchanged.

labels.name: claude
labels.description: Routes the issue to the Claude provider for execution.
notes: Existing control behavior remains unchanged.

labels.name: gemini
labels.description: Routes the issue to the Gemini provider for execution.
notes: Existing control behavior remains unchanged.

labels.name: vigilante:resume
labels.description: Requests that Vigilante resume a blocked session.
notes: Preferred namespaced control label.
Vigilante should remove it after acknowledging the resume request.

labels.name: vigilante:automerge
labels.description: Requests automatic squash merge once required checks and merge requirements are satisfied.
notes: May be applied to the issue before the PR exists or directly to the PR later.
Does not bypass branch protection, required reviews, failing checks, or merge conflicts.

labels.name: resume
notes: Keep until existing operator workflows are migrated.
